text: Cold-water immersion is superior to other common recovery modalities for recovering from muscle soreness following acute strenuous exercise.
Cold-water immersion produces similar recovery of muscular power and flexibility compared with other common recovery modalities following acute strenuous exercise.
Cold-water immersion is more effective than active recovery, contrast water therapy, and warm-water immersion for most recovery outcomes following acute strenuous exercise.
Air cryotherapy is significantly more effective than cold-water immersion for recovery of muscular strength and immediate recovery of muscular power at 1 hour post-exercise.
Water temperature and exposure duration of cold-water immersion rarely moderate recovery outcomes, suggesting a wide range of protocols may be similarly effective.
